name: Endor Labs Supply Chain Security API
what_it_does: Next-generation Software Composition Analysis (SCA) platform with REST API — using call graph reachability analysis to identify only exploitable vulnerabilities (not all CVEs), reducing noise by 80%+ while providing SBOM, license compliance, and dependency lifecycle management.
use_cases: Scanning dependencies for actually reachable/exploitable vulnerabilities (reducing CVE noise)
Generating accurate SBOMs with reachability context for compliance
Querying dependency risk scores for prioritized remediation in CI/CD pipelines
Monitoring open-source project health (maintainer activity, version staleness)
Automating PR blocking for critical reachable vulnerabilities via API policy enforcement
not_for: SAST for custom code (focus is open-source dependency analysis)
Small teams where basic Snyk/Dependabot coverage is sufficient
best_when: A security team drowning in SCA noise wants reachability-based filtering to focus remediation on genuinely exploitable vulnerabilities, with SBOM generation for enterprise compliance.
avoid_when: You need broad SAST, runtime security, or are a small team where alert volume isn't yet a problem.

auth.notes: OAuth2 with OIDC for CI/CD integration. API key for direct access. Namespace-scoped tokens. Workload identity federation for cloud CI (GitHub Actions OIDC, GCP). Scoped access for scan submission and result reading.

pricing.notes: Enterprise-only pricing. US-based startup (San Francisco). Founded 2022 — young but well-funded ($70M Series B). Former Google security team founders. Reachability analysis is key differentiator vs Snyk/Mend. GitHub, GitLab, Azure DevOps integration. Growing enterprise adoption.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for namespaces, projects, packages, scans, findings, and SBOMs. OAuth2/OIDC for CI/CD. Webhook for scan completion. Namespace-centric data model requires understanding hierarchy. Reachability context in API responses is key differentiator. Early-stage API may evolve.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Scan submission creates new scan jobs — async. Finding reads idempotent. SBOM exports create new artifacts. Project management idempotent by identifier.
known_agent_gotchas: Namespace-scoped API — must know namespace identifier for all operations
Reachability analysis adds scan latency vs simpler SCA tools
OIDC workload identity preferred over API keys for CI/CD
Early-stage company — API surface still evolving; check changelog before updates

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 certified. GDPR compliant. OIDC workload identity federation — no long-lived secrets in CI/CD. Namespace-scoped tokens. Former Google security founders — strong security DNA. US/EU data residency.
